sources.authors: Francisco Javier Ordonez and Daniel Roggen
sources.justification: Supports learning temporal representations directly from minimally processed multimodal wearable streams rather than supplying candidate-wise oracle scores.
sources.title: Deep Convolutional and LSTM Recurrent Neural Networks for Multimodal Wearable Activity Recognition

sources.authors: Roy Adams, Nazir Saleheen, Edison Thomaz, Abhinav Parate, Santosh Kumar, and Benjamin Marlin
sources.justification: Establishes event detection and temporal segmentation as joint problems over continuous wearable sensor streams.
sources.title: Hierarchical Span-Based Conditional Random Fields for Labeling and Segmenting Events in Wearable Sensor Data Streams

sources.authors: Zheng Shou, Hang Gao, Lei Zhang, Kazuyuki Miyazawa, and Shih-Fu Chang
sources.justification: Motivates separating weak bag-level supervision from explicit temporal-boundary prediction in untrimmed sequences.
sources.title: AutoLoc: Weakly-supervised Temporal Action Localization in Untrimmed Videos

sources.authors: Natalia Neverova, Christian Wolf, Graham W. Taylor, and Florian Nebout
sources.justification: Supports channel dropout during fusion so a learner remains useful when a modality is missing or corrupted.
sources.title: ModDrop: Adaptive Multi-Modal Gesture Recognition

sources.justification: Motivates bounded, availability-aware fusion rather than forcing every present modality into every prediction.
sources.title: EmbraceNet: A robust deep learning architecture for multimodal classification

sources.justification: Supports a strict training-only information boundary: auxiliary explanations may shape learned state but are unavailable at evaluation.
sources.title: A new learning paradigm: Learning using privileged information

sources.authors: Afsaneh Fazly, Afra Alishahi, and Suzanne Stevenson
sources.justification: Supports probabilistic alignment jointly with accumulation of word-meaning evidence across ambiguous situations.
sources.title: A Probabilistic Computational Model of Cross-Situational Word Learning

sources.justification: Motivates holding out combinations while retaining exposure to their atomic components, and explicitly separating atom from compound generalization.
sources.title: Measuring Compositional Generalization: A Comprehensive Method on Realistic Data

sources.authors: Rustam Ibragimov and Ulrich K. Muller
sources.justification: Supports t-based inference over a small number of independent group-level estimates; v2 therefore reduces to one averaged effect per corpus seed before inference.
sources.publication: Review of Economics and Statistics 98(1):83-96 (2016), doi:10.1162/REST_a_00545
sources.title: Inference with Few Heterogeneous Clusters

sources.authors: Ivan A. Canay, Joseph P. Romano, and Azeem M. Shaikh
sources.justification: Supports sign-flip randomization evidence when group-level estimates are symmetric or approximately symmetric; v2 reports it as corroboration rather than replacing the corpus-level estimate.
sources.title: Randomization Tests Under an Approximate Symmetry Assumption

sources.justification: Supports an intersection-union decision in which the global alternative is established only when every co-primary component rejects at the nominal level, without alpha splitting.
sources.title: Multiparameter Hypothesis Testing and Acceptance Sampling
